title: ranunculus bellulus
intro: Ranunculus bellulus, also known as the creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus bellulus include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
